Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Petitioner
Judy Hitchcock for Dolores Pineda (Trust Beneficiary)

Mr. Siracusa stated that notice was given to residual beneficiaries (nuns) which was difficult since they are in Nicaragua and their focus was all on Ms. Pineda's care. Ms. Hitchcock stated her office spoke with the sisters/nuns several times who expressed their concern about having to pay anything and indicated they wanted nothing to do with the settlement. Mr. Siracusa stated that he thinks the Court previously denied his petition because they didn't want to give a stamp of approval to not pay property taxes, he spoke with the City, that they are very sympathetic, but they cannot consider forbearance and the need of the person until a 5 year mark and they are 2 years away from the 5 year mark. Mr. Siracusa also stated that NOT selling the property would be the best economic choice for Ms. Pineda and the residual beneficiaries. In addition, Mr. Siracusa spoke of a loss of sales price if there were to be a life estate, the possibility of falling under the Ellis Act if the property is sold with Ms. Pineda remaining on the premises, and a reverse mortgage not being an option because Ms. Pineda does not own the property. Ms. Hitchcock stated that the property is not eligible for a postponement of tax due to age since the property is owned by the Trust. Counsels indicated that they have no problem with the Court adding a sentence to the proposed order stating "the Court in no way condones non-payment of property taxes." The petition to approve settlement agreement is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera, Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

The Court granted the Petition for Authority to list and sell real property, and to enter into exclusive listing agreement with real estate broker (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for (petitioner/trustee) Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked about the possibility of selling the property. Mr. Siracusa reported that there was a delinquent tax bill of $110,160.00, that they filed an appeal and were able to get it reduced to $78,635.00, that the beneficiary is 94 years old, that this is her home and she wants to remain there and that it was the settlor's intent for her to stay in the home. Mr. Siracusa explained the 4 options that were available, 1. to sell the property upon the death of Ms. Pineda, 2. sell the property and rent the unit back to Ms. Pineda at $3500.00 per month, 3. sell the property and Ms. Pineda remains there paying no rent, and 4. sell the property with a life estate to Ms. Pineda, that the preference is option 1, that options 2,3 & 4 cause loss to the residual beneficiary, that they are not asking the court to instruct the trustee to not pay the taxes, that they would like the trustee to be relieved of liability only, that the income generated is being used to pay expenses, that there is a $40,000.00 reserve in case there are problems with the property, that Ms. Pineda is not currently receiving distributions, that if the property is sold she could be evicted, that the only way to assure that she is able to remain is to sell the property with the retain for life clause, that the City & County of San Francisco will not be harmed by this, that they will benefit by the penalties that will accrue, that the risk to the trustee is a surcharge, that counsel can provide additional information if requested and that the right result is for Ms. Pineda to remain in the house. The petition for instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with (petitioner/trustee) Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked counsel about the request for legal authority regarding outstanding debts. Mr. Siracusa reported that they are not asking the court to direct them not to pay taxes, the issue is that there is no money to pay the taxes, that they want authorization not to pay to avoid a surcharge at a later date, that it would relieve the trustee of liability, that they called the City Attorney's office to negotiate, were referred to the property tax department, they were then told that they could not negotiate until the property is in foreclosure which will be in 2020, that they could sell the property but it would be very distressing to Ms. Pineda, that they would get much less for the property due to the tenants that are there, that they do not have authority to relocate Ms. Pineda and that they have an appeal coming up on 11/3/15 to try and get the base reduced but that it is in the best interest of Ms. Pineda to keep the property. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Maria Pinell Kanewske.

Mr. Bridgman apologized to the Court noting that he somehow thought that he could file the petition while traveling in Europe, that he has the petition with him, and that he is ready to file it today. In addition, Mr. Bridgman stated that his client has had some difficulties in dealing with the life beneficiary, her Aunt, and that his client wishes to resign. The Court suggested that Mr. Bridgman talk with some professionals, file the second account today, amend it later if necessary, and then file a petition to substitute the Trustee. The Court stated that if Mr. Bridgman would provide proof that the second account was filed that today's status would go off calendar.

Mr. Bridgman provided proof that the second account had been e-filed. The matter was taken off calendar.

Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Garcia for and with Dolores Pineda. James Bridgman for and with Lourdes Kanewske.

Mr. Bridgman stated that there would be no net income until two years. The matter was taken under submission. The Court ordered that proof of filing of the $20,000.00 bond be filed by September 30, 2010. Status of filing of bond was set for October 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An appearance is required on that date if the bond is not filed.
